items.title: Function pointers and callbacks: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? Casting any function address to the requested pointer type makes an incompatible callback call safe.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. The actual function and pointer call type must be compatible; a cast can silence a diagnostic but cannot create a valid calling convention.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. The actual function and pointer call type must be compatible; a cast can silence a diagnostic but cannot create a valid calling convention.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: The actual function and pointer call type must be compatible; a cast can silence a diagnostic but cannot create a valid calling convention.
Casting any function address to the requested pointer type makes an incompatible callback call safe.

answer.explanation: The actual function and pointer call type must be compatible; a cast can silence a diagnostic but cannot create a valid calling convention.

items.title: Function pointers and callbacks: executable application
items.prompt: Read an operation character plus two integers, select a compatible add or multiply callback, invoke it through a typed function pointer, and print the result.
